rationale: Live-action UGC testimonial bypasses Crowd City's biggest creative asset — the visceral, instantly-readable visual of a swarming stickman crowd consuming rivals — so the hook does almost no work to communicate the core loop. The muted bedroom palette and "reached level 55" progression framing also misalign with a hypercasual game that has no meaningful long-term progression and sells on 5-second satisfaction clips, not retention bragging. Audience skew (broad casual) is the only mild positive; recommend skipping unless reshot with on-screen gameplay B-roll showing the crowd-swarm payoff.
